title: Level 672

{{Class 1|hexagon=1|color-h=10|color-s=10%|header=Survival Difficulty: Class|class=1|safety=Barren|security=Lonely|entity=[[Entity 112|The Armless]]}}'''Level 672''', known as "Lifeless Slopes" or "Monotony," is the 673rd level of [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]]. == General Description == {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Level 672 AGAIN.jpg|hovertext=Lifeless Slopes.|caption=A field.}} '''Level 672''' is a big, barren island of dead, abnormal slopes and hills filled with smooth, tall, gray dead grass. Occasionally, one might discover trees and dead bushes. Most plants are typically scarce in resources like fruit. The sky is perpetually stuck in a state of cloudiness and rain. Unlike other places, however, rain is very heavy, often leading to mild floods. Most of the level contains a peculiar quality that makes it grayer. Noticeably, '''Level 672''' has too little sunlight for plants to undergo photosynthesis, leading to a lack of resources and the food chain breaking. '''Level 672''''s temperatures average between -35°C at its coldest and 10°C at its hottest. The level's winds reach speeds around 8 to 40 mph, commonly experiencing a fresh breeze or, on rarer occasions, a strong gale. Temperature seems to impact '''Level 672''' quite significantly, changing cloud formations, grass patterns, animal life, sky color, etc. Usually, on colder days, the sky is clear and a grayish white, plants rot away, and present life forms die from either hypothermia or starvation. Meanwhile, on hotter days, rain might happen, the sky becomes gray and cloudy, and life is mildly possible. Despite the level's capability to have different seasons, these are virtually irrelevant, only resulting in mild temperature and entity behavior changes. There are a few lakes spread around the island. These often take the shape of nearby items found in '''Level 672''', like berries, bushes, etc. These lakes all house crystalline water. This water is inappropriate for any human contact. It is toxic for ingestion, and anything in it will sink rapidly. This odd water seems to draw nearby entities closer. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Level 672 New.jpg|hovertext=Lifeless Slopes.|caption=Another field.}} The island is composed of three main natural formations: hills, depressions, and elevations. Depressions are the deepest, and elevations are higher up. A phenomenon is observed in '''Level 672''' in which temperature changes wildly based on elevation. Depressions are often extremely hot, while elevations are freezing cold. Six feet under '''Level 672''''s surface soil is an expansive and deserted network of tunnels, made up of materials like metal and stone. There are food and drinks trashed across the tunnels. Upon closer observation, there are scratches, chips, and crack marks on the walls. Parts of the tunnels are blocked out by dirt and gravel, possibly caused by earthquakes or unstable architecture. == Present Life Forms == '''Level 672''' has a minimal entity count due to the lack of resources. [[Entity 72|Foams]], [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 75|Jantlers]], and [[Entity 190|Plastic Worms]] are some entities found on this level. In addition to entities, the most common life forms in '''Level 672''' are grass, oak trees, and bushes. === [[Entity 72|Foams]] === [[Entity 72|Foams]] are a rare sight in '''Level 672''', due to the level's barren layout. They typically patrol around the surface searching for food, which more likely than not ends up being tree bark, grass, or leaves. Foams have been observed to save more food at this level. When a [[Entity 72|Foam]] manages to enter the tunnel network, it behaves more erratically, frantically eating the food trashed across the floor or unfortunate [[Entity 190|Plastic Worms]]. Upon eating, [[Entity 72|Foams]] group up and station themselves in the ceiling. === [[Entity 3|Smilers]] === [[Entity 3|Smilers]] are an endangered species in '''Level 672'''. They inhabit the tunnels, hopelessly seeking food. Most of them starve before finding nourishment. === [[Entity 190|Plastic Worms]] === [[Entity 190|Plastic Worms]] will slither around the tunnels, catching pieces of trash and food and storing them in their nests. Their nests are typically built in holes in the walls or dark corners. [[Entity 190|Plastic Worms]] commonly group up around corpses, eating off their flesh, or stealing from others and quickly making an escape. This practice normally leads up to big fights in these areas. === [[Entity 112|The Armless]] === [[Entity 112|The Armless]] are the main entities and absolute rulers in '''Level 672'''. [[Entity 112|The Armless]] inhabit all across the level, wandering around, searching for food. When two instances come across each other, they trade important information and sometimes even food with each other, parting ways shortly after. [[Entity 112|The Armless]] typically go to the tunnel network to meet up and get food; when they are just exploring the level or looking for humans, they wander the surface. Due to its barren layout, '''Level 672''' is the only level in which different instances of [[Entity 112|The Armless]] cooperate, and consequently, the only level to be dominated by them. One of the few unspoken rules in '''Level 672''' is the prohibition of human entry. [[Entity 112|The Armless]] are prompted to kill humans they come across for the sake of the community. == Colonies, Outposts, and Settlements == There are no human settlements within '''Level 672'''. Although [[Entity 112|the Armless]] have formed many peaceful outposts throughout the entirety of the level, these outposts come together as one large community. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances: === Entrance to '''Level 672''' is strictly forbidden, as most result in death. Groups have made an effort to obfuscate the entries of such. === Exits: === * Laying on a patch of yellow grass leads one to [[Level 671]]. * Approximating to an [[Entity 112|Armless]] outpost may lead to [[Level 667]]. * Relaxing on the bodies of water may lead one to [[Level 602]]. * A [[Phenomenon 200|Sunny Night]] on '''Level 672''' may randomly no-clip one into [[Level 600]]. * Stepping on a dead bush may take one to [[Level 606]]. * No-clipping into snow in winter will lead one to [[Level 616]]. {{Redirect|Next=673|Prev=671}}
}}}
